posts.caption:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”
-
Photography: @jonathanfrantini
Styling: @raeboxer
Creative Director: @lauragenninger
Story: @lynettesaid
Hair: @shioritakahashi
Makeup: @thom.walker
Manicure: Chisato Yamamoto @chisatochee
Entertainment Director: @angcutt
Visual Director: @natashalunnwatkins

Production: @thearcadeproduction
Set design: @tilly_power33
posts.alt_text: What makes American style so unique? As we celebrated the 250th anniversary of the United States last week, the question feels particularly timely. From early tailoring traditions dating back to the 1800s, like the button-down-collar shirt from Brooks Brothers, to the logo-centric aesthetics of the ’90s, led by brands such as Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger, and Ralph Lauren, the essentials of American style capture a history of utility, branding, and cultural movements. At the link in our bio, take a deep dive into Bazaar’s running list of 25 American style essentials from both past and present that continue to resonate.

posts.caption: This is what fairytales are made of. @matthieu_blazy had all of #ParisCoutureWeek talking about shoes. What do you think? Would you channel your inner Cinderella by wearing these magical moments?

posts.caption: You don’t get a lot of new names on the couture calendar. But this season, everyone is buzzing about @standing_ground_, a made-to-order label from Michael Stewart, who made his couture debut this week at Paris’s Irish Embassy. In cultivating his signature technique of casing beadwork in sheer fabric which he then applies to his sculpturally draped garments, Stewart has quietly gained a word-of-mouth client base over the past few years. Tap the link in bio to learn more about the new designer everyone is talking about this fashion week.

posts.caption:
Only @irisvanherpen could design couture like this>>
For years, van Herpen has blurred the line between fashion, and fantasy. From the ethereal ‘Airo’ dress worn by @eileengu at the 2026 Met Gala, her silhouettes seem to defy the laws of gravity.
Her Haute Couture Fall/Winter 2026–2027 also highlighted the  glass bubbles floated through the runway, as well as a first for fashion with a ‘plasma ‘ dress.
Van Herpen describes it as, “This collection gives form to forces that rarely reveal themselves. I do not seek to explain them; instead, they heighten our awareness of the unknown, reminding us of the mysteries that lie within this multiverse.”
📷: @stefanoscon , @irisvanherpen
posts.alt_text:
Sometimes, the coolest styling move is taking something away. The buttonless pullover shirt, known in Japan as the "skipper" shirt, strips back closures in favor of an easy, effortless silhouette that feels equal parts classic and quietly progressive.⁠
⁠
From @maisonmargiela taped polo and Working's quarter-zip to elevated takes by @comoli.official, @blurhms_official, Kaptain Sunshine, and @ancellm_official, the skipper shirt proves that a small design tweak can completely change the feel of a wardrobe staple. It's simple, understated, and, somehow, even cooler because of it.

posts.caption:
In an industry obsessed with the beige purgatory of minimalism, @giulianocalza prefers the art of doing “the absolute most."
As the founder and creative engine behind @gcdswear, Calza has spent the last decade proving that you can build a million-dollar empire on a foundation of pink vinyl, Hello Kitty and unadulterated aura maxxing. But to dismiss GCDS as mere "kitsch" is to miss the point entirely. Underneath it all lies a designer who is as dedicated to the codes as any heritage house.
For Hypebae’s 10th anniversary, we’re spotlighting creative directors who are celebrating their own. In this installment, Hypebae’s EIC @caitcmo speaks with Calza about his proudest moments and how he continues to subvert Italian luxury into something unmistakably his.
Credit: @benjasalazar

Oh, to be tanning on your roof in a @nii.hai bikini and wedges. 😍 The London-based fashion brand’s latest lookbook was shot by Diego Pérez de la Cruz, and there couldn’t be a more perfect way to introduce its expansion into swimwear.  Which look is your favorite?
Imagery: Diego Pérez de la Cruz, Nii HAi

For creative director @matthieu_blazy’s second Couture show with @chanelofficial, the House brought a fairytale fantasy to life. Following his woodland inspired Couture debut in January, Blazy continued the dreamlike narrative with a collection filled with elegance. Set against a runway of twisted vines and lush florals, models embodied the enchanting vision with every look. ✨
Which look was your favorite?
Images: Getty

Created by @rotatebirgerchristensen at its Copenhagen headquarters, this custom ensemble for @zaralarsson took more than 300 hours to complete. Embellished with 3,000 @swarovski crystals, hand-dyed low rise denim shorts are paired with a halterneck top crafted from cascading, color-graded chains.
Coupled with a signature makeup look from Larsson's close collaborator @sophiasinot, this look is going straight into our summer outfit inspo moodboards.
Images: ROTATE
Styling and Creative Direction: Caterina Ospina @cateoniwiki
Photographer: Robin Bøe @robinboee

@zendaya’s latest look is fresh off the runway! 🤩 The star stepped out in a @schiaparelli dress that walked the Paris Couture runway just hours ago. Zendaya has provided us with amazing press tour looks all summer, but this one proves that no one is outworking her and @luxurylaw.
Imagery: Getty

We’re into the knockout rounds now, but there’s still time to get your national pride-infused fits off before the tournament ends. If the country you’re rooting and your favorite players are already homeward bound, no need to worry. All the fashion from this summer of soccer is here to stay.
We saw the suggestions on our first list, and our ears perked up. Swipe to see some of the football-forward brands you put us onto, and a few new names, too.
Images: Courtesy of Brands

Summer doesn’t arrive all at once. It unfolds in moods, moments, and memories. ☀️🌿
@ahsaassy_ steps into the season’s softest chapter, where easy silhouettes and unhurried afternoons become a reminder that slowing down is its own kind of luxury.
In collaboration with @aurelia_womenswear , this explores the evolving moods of summer, from nature’s quiet embrace to the intensity of the afternoon sun, and the effortless cool of golden evenings.✨
This is a celebration of the season in all its shades, beginning with the calm before the heat, where style isn’t about standing out, but feeling completely at home in your own skin.

posts.caption:
So close...
Egypt leaves the 2026 FIFA World Cup in round 16, the furthest the nation has ever progressed, finishing just one goal away from eliminating the reigning champions.
The Pharaohs captured the hearts of football fans around the world, from Africa, the Middle East, and beyond.
This generational run marks the beginning of a new era for Egyptian football, with Mo Salah proving himself a captain worthy of the title.
Photos: Getty Images
@leomessi is the only player in World Cup history to have scored in six consecutive knockout matches.

posts.caption:
@fiat is bringing the ultra-cute Topolino mini city car to the United States, and it's pretty affordable. But there's a catch.⁠
⁠
According to @roadandtrack, the Fiat Topolino is coming to the US market, priced at a very affordable $15,000 USD. At launch there will be two variants: the standard model with opposing hinged doors and a glass roof, as well as a "Dolcevita" edition with rope barriers for doors and a rollable soft top. Both will only come in Verde Vita green, with the potential for more colors in the future.⁠
⁠
Performance isn't really a factor for this car: the electric motor up front is only capable of 8 horsepower, and will top speed at under 20mph. Range as well is limited to just 46 miles, so don't expect to take any road trip in it. Even a weekend getaway to the beach might be difficult. But if you need something to swing by the supermarket or grab coffee in, this 1,073 lb. "city car" will do the trick. Think of it as a two-seat scooter with a roof.⁠
⁠
Creature comforts? Not many. There's a USB-C charging port, a portable speaker, a phone mount, back up camera and LED lights. ⁠
⁠
Here's the big catch though: it's technically not even road legal in the States as of now. Reports state that a conversion kit will arrive soon after release to allow it to be sold as a "low speed vehicle" for American roads. The kit will also raise the top speed to 25, but naturally that will lower the range a bit. ⁠
⁠
Fiat is currently taking $2,500 USD deposits for a Topolino right now at its official website.⁠
⁠
Photos: Stellantis
posts.alt_text:
The McLaren M6GT pretty much started it all for the brand, and MSO just recreated it to honor its founder, Bruce McLaren.⁠
⁠
McLaren is going deep into its own archive for Goodwood, unveiling a one-off M6GT restoration built by McLaren Special Operations (MSO) that brings founder Bruce McLaren's original road-car ambition full circle. Constructed from a period-built M6A racer chassis using original body molds unearthed in the UK, the recreation pairs a period-correct small-block V8 with "camel hump" cylinder heads to stay true to spec, right down to imperial-era bearings sourced specifically for the restoration. Attention to detail is key here: a hand-turned solid walnut gear knob, aerospace-grade dome rivets installed by a specialist, and a cockpit lifted directly from a 1970s M6A race car makes this more than just a restomod. The car is lastly dinished in a bespoke cream "Colnbrook" white with green interior accents, a subtle nod to Bruce's 1966 M2B F1 livery. ⁠
⁠
The car will be on display during the Goodwood Festival of Speed this coming weekend.⁠
⁠
Photos: McLaren

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
Someone play "lacy" by #OliviaRodrigo please, because #MartaKostyuk's lace kit from #Wimbledon is all we can think about today.
While many stars and players show up to the games looking their best, we simply could not take our eyes off of @martakostyuk. Dubbed the "Marta Dress" by @wilsontennis, the kit is described as "a transcendent two-piece crafted in all-new technical lace." We are simply obsessed.
📷: @gettyimages

Se gli si domanda che film gli piaccia guardare, @TravisScott risponde «qualsiasi cosa di Wes Anderson, Robert Rodriguez o dei fratelli Coen». Anche se ultimamente al protagonista della copertina di Luglio 2026 de @LOfficielItalia piace «scoprire nuovi film con sceneggiature così belle che ti catturano completamente». posts.alt_text:
Un rovescio a una mano, raro nel tennis di oggi, e un gioco elegante e variato: @Lore_Musetti, 24 anni, ha aperto il 2026 conquistando il quinto posto del ranking ATP, prima che un infortunio alla gamba sinistra lo costringesse a rinunciare al Roland Garros. Global ambassador di #BottegaVeneta, il tennista si racconta a @LOfficielItalia, ritratto dagli scatti di @RaffGrosso e con lo styling di @Fernando.Echeverria_, vestendo un total look della collezione #Fall2026, firmato dalla direttrice creativa @Louise_Trotter_. Tra scaramanzie da spogliatoio, il bronzo olimpico di Parigi, l'ammirazione per il tennis di Alcaraz e la passione per la cucina: «Colpire la pallina, il suono che fa quando la colpisco: è questo che mi rende felice». Leggi l’intervista a cura di @Fabia.DiDrusco al link in bio e sul nuovo numero de @LOfficielItalia, ora in edicola.

@lore_musetti
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo
Fashion Director @therealgiuliamartinelli

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!
The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia’ from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.
From music to film to fashion, Scott’s creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‘Rolling Loud’ and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.
Yet at the center of it all remains the music. “I’ve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,” Scott says. “I’ve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.”
Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
There’s no duality in the way Babil Khan (@babil.i.k) exists; what you see is what you get. A bundle of energy and a force of conviction, under the spotlight, but especially outside of it. In fact, now there’s an added sheen of alignment, and it looks great on him.
From opening up about navigating the industry to finally finding his voice, our latest cover star unpacks the family legacy and identity.
Head to the link in bio to read the entire cover story.
Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)
Photographer: Chandrahas Prabhu (@chandrahas_prabhu)
Styling: Who Wore What When (@who_wore_what_when)
Assisted by: Shubham J (@d.shubham_j)
Interviewed by: Sushri Sahu (@sushri_sahu)
Makeup: Mohak Chavan (@muamohak) and Aniket Kamble (@aniket_cz)
Hair by: Tej (@hairby_tej)
Artist Reputation Management: Hardly Anonymous Communications (@hardlyanonymouspr)
Jacket & Pants - NM Studio (@nm_design_studio)
Shirt - Rare Rabbit - (@rarerabbit_in)
Shoes - Ecco (@ecco)
Sunglasses - Marc Jacobs (@marcjacobs)
[LSA Cover Star, Babil Khan, Bollywood, Cover Story, July 2026]
posts.alt_text:
Anuv Jain’s (@anuvjain) music is what feelings sound like. Love, passion, calm, longing, and so much more… Words may fail, but you’ll always find an Anuv Jain song, or a lyric that resonates with you. This is the power of music, and what Anuv’s rise has always been about.

Be it Husn, Baarishein, Alag Aasmaan, or his latest, Arz Kiya, Anuv’s music has had a chokehold on the youth and has given words to emotions felt by crowds.

He claims to be a yearner, someone who likes to live in the past at times, feels too deeply, and expresses himself with intention. This Music Day, we raise a toast to his music, his success, and his love for the craft.

Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)
Photographer: Sasha Jairam (@sashajairam)
Fashion Editor: Neelangana Vasudeva (@neelangana)
Produced by: Chirrag Guha (@guha.archie)
Interviewed by: Tanzim Pardiwalla (@pepper_tan) 
Junior Stylist: Karnika Kabra (@karnika_kabra) 
Hair and Makeup by: Chriselle Ann Baptista (@chrissybaps)
Assisted by: Pinky Roy (@pinkyroy9467)
Artist Reputation Management: Aurum Entertainment (@aurum_entpr) 

Wardrobe partner: Pepe Jeans (@pepejeansindia)

Accessories from @ishhaara via @ascend.rohank 

[LSA Cover Star, Anuv Jain, Music, Musician, June 2026]

Fresh off the release of his 9 song long sophomore album; ‘A Little Larger Than The Entire Universe,’ Chennai-Goa based musician JBABE (@joshurbanned) is fronting Spotify’s global radar, and sounds more mature than ever before. The album as the name suggests, expands the absurd world of JBABE, both sonically and in its creative vision - the poetry inspired naming however stays the same.
At the link in bio Chirrag Guha (@guha.archie) chats with the artist on his creative roots and inspirations, what it really means to be niche in today’s Indian musical landscape today, lust; god and how he’s celibate now.

posts.caption:
His work has brought him to countless destinations across the globe, but @TravisScott still has one major stop left on his bucket list. “I hit every continent on the last tour except Antarctica,” he says. “It’s kind of crazy.”
For Scott, travel is about more than seeing new places—it’s about expanding perspective, and he encourages leaning into exploration whenever possible. “Go touch earth and go actually interact with this world,” he says. “It could change your mindset.”
Read more about Scott’s adventures at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!

The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia’ from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.

From music to film to fashion, Scott’s creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‘Rolling Loud’ and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.

Yet at the center of it all remains the music. “I’ve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,” Scott says. “I’ve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.”

Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.

Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
The 2026 July/August issue is here!
At the center of this year’s Summer Entertainment Issue is @TravisScott, who opens up about working on the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia,’ the travels and experiences fueling his next chapter, and making his acting debut as part of a star-studded ensemble in Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey.’
As much as we love a blockbuster with a stacked A-list cast, championing fresh talent is always top of mind. Inside the issue, we spotlight eight rising stars whose work this summer demands your attention, from ‘Outer Banks’ star @MadisonBaileyBabe to Avantika of ‘Not Suitable for Work’ to @sashacalle of ‘Sugar.’
It’s also a blockbuster time for literary escapes, as the rise of #BookTok turns reading into an unexpected status symbol that’s shaping summer travel. Looking ahead to the Fall collections, we explore the age-old question of whether sex still sells, given the state of undress on the runway, red carpet, and social feeds in 2026. Stay tuned for the issue hitting newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
For our July/August issue, we caught up with Avantika to discuss her first leading TV role: portraying a celebrity stylist’s assistant in Hulu’s New York–centric sitcom ‘Not Suitable for Work.’ The experience was both daunting and deeply relatable. “So much of life in the city—especially as a young person—is hustle culture,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. “There are many opportunities for love and romance, but there’s also this feeling that you have to compromise or sacrifice those things in order to advance your career. Navigating the push and pull of work-life balance is not inherently very sexy. But I find it exciting.”
Living with what she calls “radical optimism,” the 21-year-old—who built a career in Indian cinema before her American film debut in ‘Spin’ in 2021—is embracing her Hollywood chapter. “I used to dream of days like this,” she says. “I think most young women who come from a very rich culture and then move somewhere like America are told that there are very clear parameters for what they can and can’t be. I hope that, through the characters that I play, young Indian girls see a real and emboldening representation of women who go beyond that.”
Read more of our conversation with Avantika at the link in bio.
Editor-in-Chief of @lofficielusa @caroline_grosso⁠

posts.caption:
A pop star without an unforgettable wardrobe? As if. The biggest performers have always understood that fashion is just as much a part of the spectacle as the music itself. With every era came a new wave of artists serving chart-topping hits and equally iconic looks, proving that style has always been part of the performance.
The ‘90s ushered in the age of the diva. From @Madonna to #WhitneyHouston, #MariahCarey, and #CélineDion—to name just a few—the decade was defined by powerhouse vocals and equally memorable fashion moments. Their wardrobes struck the perfect balance between polished glamour and larger-than-life personality, cementing them as style icons on and off the stage.
Then came Y2K, a maximalist era that doubled down on bold self-expression. Colorful looks, low-rise silhouettes, bedazzled everything, and, of course, cargo pants became staples of the pop star uniform. The trend cycle only kept accelerating, making way for the 2010s, when “more is more” became the unofficial dress code. Think @katyperry’s neon hair, the candy-coated aesthetic of ‘Teenage Dream,’ and the flower crowns that became a signature on nearly every red carpet.
Head to the link in bio to see how pop star style has evolved over the decades—and where the next era of fashion is headed.
Photos Getty Images
#fashionhistory #popstarstyle

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra)

Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)

posts.alt_text:
If you’ve been on a wedding jewellery buying spree you know the drill—it’s an exercise fraught with visiting spiffy places that work on silence, nods, smiles and quiet conversation. No place for fun, let alone dining while looking at jewellery busts and necklace displays. But Tiara Dhody’s (@tiaradhody) new space Treasures Cafe, on Peddar Road, is breaking that mould with a refreshing narrative—it blends the jewellery shopping with food, fun and friendly banter.

Come in, browse around and tuck into her family recipes, served with a side of jewellery. Who wouldn’t like a little happy bling over a meal! 

📍Treasures Cafe, Mumbai
By Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)

Link in bio🔗
—

posts.caption:
Gen Z is making sure nobody’s just standing around waiting for the next function at an Indian wedding.
We’re talking cricket tournaments, DIY perfume bars, IV drip lounges, cocktails served in syringes, dancing gorilla mascots, sunset pool parties and underground-style music nights. And that’s just the beginning.
Gen Z doesn’t want a wedding they simply attend—they want one they can play, film, post and remember. The vibe? Less “please take your seats,” more “everyone’s having fun.”
Read the full story via the link in bio. 🔗
By Kratagya Rathore (@kratagya_rathore)

posts.alt_text:
#ManifestCover 

There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.

For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.

“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.

Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”

Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.

Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

Somewhere between fashion and functionality, wearable beauty accessories have quietly become the internet’s cutest obsession. Think all your products doubling up as bag charms. They’re practical, yes—but let’s be honest, they’re also just ridiculously adorable.

We’ve rounded up the wearable beauty accessories proving that sometimes, the prettiest things are also the most practical.

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Singapore has been named the world’s most expensive city for the wealthy for the fourth consecutive year.
According to the latest Julius Baer Global Wealth & Lifestyle Report, the city-state retained the top spot thanks to the high cost of luxury real estate, cars, watches, jewellery, and premium lifestyle experiences. Zurich ranked second, followed by Monaco, with Hong Kong and London completing the global top five. Asia-Pacific now accounts for half of the world’s 10 most expensive cities for high-net-worth individuals.
Despite its high costs, Singapore continues to attract the world’s wealthiest thanks to its political stability, strong economy, world-class infrastructure, and reputation as one of the safest places to live.
#Luxurious

posts.caption:
The world’s biggest sporting events have become some of luxury’s most powerful marketing platforms.
From Louis Vuitton crafting trophy trunks for the FIFA World Cup and Formula 1 to Rolex’s long-standing partnerships with Wimbledon, Formula 1, and golf’s biggest championships, luxury brands are using global sport to showcase craftsmanship, heritage, and prestige to millions of fans worldwide. These collaborations have transformed major tournaments into global stages where sport, fashion, watches, and luxury seamlessly come together.
Here are the luxury brands behind some of the world’s biggest sporting events.

posts.caption:
Luxury retail is no longer just about shopping. It’s about architecture, art, and creating unforgettable experiences.
The prestigious Prix Versailles architecture awards have unveiled the world’s most beautiful luxury stores for 2026, recognizing flagship boutiques that blur the line between retail, culture, and design. From Dior’s sculptural flagship in Beijing and Cartier’s architectural landmark in Miami to Tiffany & Co.‘s striking Ginza boutique and Issey Miyake’s minimalist New York space, these destinations are redefining what a luxury store can be.
Here are the most beautiful luxury stores in the world for 2026.

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra’s Fall/Winter 2026 Couture collection, Devi, transformed the Paris runway into a celebration of India’s artistic and spiritual heritage.
Drawing inspiration from the ancient temples of Ajanta, Ellora, and Gandhara, the collection reinterpreted stone carvings, celestial dancers, and sacred iconography through extraordinary hand embroidery, sculptural silhouettes, and intricate craftsmanship. Ceremonial headpieces created with traditional artisans elevated the presentation, blurring the line between couture, art, and architecture.
More than a couture collection, Devi was a tribute to India’s cultural legacy, showcasing how heritage craftsmanship continues to shape the future of global luxury fashion.
@rahulmishra_7 #RahulMishra

posts.caption:
At the Dior couture show on Monday in Paris, there was an elephant the room: the still-secret design of the Dior wedding dress Taylor Swift had worn a mere three days earlier.
Was it more classic — i.e., cliché — Dior, or the more current, somewhat challenging Dior? Was it princess-y? Prom-like? Kinda conceptual? Was it, in other words, more like the image that Swift has been presenting to the world, at least onstage, or more like the one that the newish designer Jonathan Anderson has been building for the brand?
The show didn’t necessarily offer any clues. But it did demonstrate why Swift might have thought Dior was the right brand for her in the first place. Anderson's work is "increasingly both notably intricate and apparently relaxed, a tough balancing act to pull off," wrote Vanessa Friedman, chief fashion critic for The New York Times. At the link in our bio, see more photos from @dior's couture show and read @vvfriedman's full review. Photos by Emma Da Silva/@apnews, @simbarashecha
posts.alt_text:
Introducing the most stylish people of 2026, so far. As we reflect on the past six months of style, our list of stylish people is not merely an appreciation of really great clothes, though there are plenty of those. It is also a snapshot of pop culture, viral moments and figures who commanded the public’s attention (and dressed the part).

Some ignited discourse, and discord, with a single look. Others used fashion to manifest some element of their identity or as an exuberant expression of their personality.

The great fashion writer Kennedy Fraser once argued that style is a moving target; it is also “individualistic” and “reckless,” she said. Some looks may not be for you, but that’s the point. At the link in our bio, see the full list of the most stylish people so far this year. Photos by Matt Slocum/@apnews, The Hapa Blonde/GC Images, Jeopardy Productions, @poupayphoto, @vincentalban1, @vnina, Denis Balibouse/Reuters, TheStewartofNY/GC Images, Mario Anzuoni/Reuters, Cameron Browne/NBAE, via Getty Images, Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via @apnews

posts.caption:
The nude or minimal nail aesthetic is everywhere in 2026, from runways to street wear and even a “Love Story” plotline. Nail salons and polish brands have reported growing preferences for nude and pink polish, while some people are skipping polish altogether. For some, the trend has become a rallying cry against complex beauty rituals. For others, it’s a way for the privileged few to show their wealth.
The look has become a status symbol, for everyone from influencers like Alix Earle, Paige Lorenze and Tinx, who all wore it in recent weeks; to perennial it girls like Zoë Kravitz and the Olsen twins; to Marc Jacobs, who sent models down a runway this year with nude nails at New York Fashion Week. When did the trend start and why? Tap the link in our bio to read more about why bare nails are everywhere. Photo by Stephen Lovekin/Shutterstock

posts.caption:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in an elaborately planned wedding with garden themes and plenty of personalized “T&T” logo details. Both were wearing Dior, though pictures have not been released yet.
Adam Sandler officiated a ceremony where the couple read their own vows. The couple’s brothers stood by their sides as the man of honor and best man.
Though few details have been confirmed by the couple, a picture was beginning to emerge of what the most star-studded night in New York in recent memory looked like. What was the theme of the décor? What was on the menu? At the link in our bio, read what we know about @taylorswift and @killatrav's wedding. Photo by @poupayphoto

posts.caption:
This year’s Essence Festival of Culture in New Orleans drew attendees from all over the U.S., looking for what Essence promises: a singular cultural landscape and cameos from beloved celebrities. Michelle Obama, the former first lady, discussed the politics of perception, the actress Keke Palmer taught a Pilates class and Cardi B, Monica and Babyface all performed.
The festival, which is usually held over the Fourth of July weekend in New Orleans, coincided with the 250th anniversary of the country’s formation. While nationwide many Americans commemorated Independence Day, at the Superdome in downtown New Orleans, some Black women honored it in their own way.
“As a Black woman, my celebration of my existence in this country looks a bit different,” said Melissa C. Potter, an attendee of the festival. “There would be no other place I would prefer to be to celebrate being Black in America.” Tap the link in our bio to see more from inside the festival. Photos by Miles Diggs, @ek_the_pj, Aaron J. Thornton/Getty Images

posts.caption:
For 250 years, the American dream has been fodder for a potent national brand.
That dream, defined by the founders as “life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness,” has been reimagined by each generation and wave of immigrants. But what does it actually look like? One way to understand the promise of this country is to look at what people have worn while chasing it.
At the link in our bio, learn how fashion has evolved alongside the American dream since 1776. Visuals by William Clarke; National Portrait Gallery, Smithsonian Institution, Topical Press Agency/Getty Images, Heritage Art/Heritage Images, via Getty Images, Getty Images, Bettmann/Getty Images, @jimwilson125, Hulu

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness
posts.alt_text:
FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more

posts.alt_text:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.

Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.

With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.

Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha

#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness

posts.caption:
When a woman decides to chase her dreams in India, loads of hurdles await her on her way. Be it society’s conservatism, stereotypes, or even families who preserve old misogynistic traditions. Hina Bano did not let her religion win over her ambitions, and she is now part of the Indian senior women's hockey team.
At the age of two, Hina lost her father. She was raised by her maternal grandfather, who was completely against women playing sports. Hina suppressed her passion for hockey until she met her coach, who encouraged her to play sports.
In a conversation with Shethepeople, Hina Bano talks about choosing sports against her family’s wish and shares how she keeps herself motivated on tough days.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full interview by @_shivanilamba
#hockey #sports Hina Bano, Indian hockey team, Indian sports, women in sports, trending, viral, explore, ambitions, conservativism, patriarchy, feminism, societal norms, stereotypes

posts.caption:
Mehek and Sahil met on a dating app and engaged in heavy ‘love bombing’ even before their second date. Suddenly, everything stopped, and he became cold and distant, leading them into a ‘situation-ship’. Mehek realised he was ‘benching’ her and chatting with other women on the same app. Sahil was ‘bread-crumbing’ her just to keep her hooked. When Mehek finally asked for clarity on where they stood, Sahil didn't respond and ‘ghosted’ her. When did love turn into a confusing vocabulary test?
From ‘love’ and ‘marriage’, ‘break up’ and ‘divorce’ to ‘open relationships’ and ‘live-in relationships’ to Gen Z terms like ghosting, situation-ships, benching, etc., relationship terms have evolved in the age of dating apps and social media. Dating in this age is more about labels.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more

posts.caption:
There is a tendency to flatten Punjabi music into one loud glossy image with the aesthetics of racing cars, alcohol, and women as background props. But Punjabi folk traditions have always been far more layered than that. Sit in a proper pind wedding, hear the dholki pick up, watch the giddha circle tighten, and then someone steps forward and says, “ni mainu kehn de!” (let me speak).
And when she does, everyone listens. Punjabi boliyan are not just songs; they are social texts. They are where women have historically said what could not always be said elsewhere. If feminism is about voice, then this has always been one of its oldest and most organic forms.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more.
#giddha #traditional punjabi boliyan, giddha, punjabi weddings, satire, songs, patriarchy, feminism, folk songs, Punjabi music, aesthetics, viral, trending, explore

posts.caption:
@janhvikapoor walked into her sister Anshula’s pheras in a custom Anamika Khanna lehenga that felt less like couture and more like a soft, golden secret — ivory-on-ivory, hand-embroidered, lit like something out of an old family photograph nobody’s seen yet.
The blouse alone could stop a room — resham and zari florals climbing across a deep-V, scalloped edges instead of a clean hem, like it was finished by hand and not by design. The dupatta wasn’t pinned into place. It was left to fall, loose over one arm, moving with her instead of around her.
And the jewellery didn’t just accessorise the look — it haunted it, in the most beautiful way. A peridot-and-gold choker sitting like a secret at the collarbone. A hair chain cascading from the ear into the hairline, catching the light every time she turned. Stacked antique-gold bangles, a haathphool draped like lace across her knuckles, a clutch embroidered to echo the lehenga petal for petal.
This wasn’t dressing up for a wedding. This was dressing like the moment mattered.
#JustSpreehIt

posts.caption:
Everyone treats the dupatta as an afterthought — pinned at the last minute, draped the same way every bride before her has done it. But the drape is what photographs from every angle, moves in every video, and frames the face in every close-up.
The double-layer drape: two dupattas in contrasting fabric or colour, one sheer over one solid, layered for depth you can actually see.
The belted waist drape: cinched at the waist with a jeweled belt before falling open behind — structured, editorial, nothing accidental about it.
The off-shoulder cascade: entirely off one shoulder and left to fall down one side, the other shoulder completely bare.
The halo veil drape: pinned in a soft circular shape around the head before trailing down the back, dupatta and veil merged into a single silhouette.
The asymmetric swirl: wrapped diagonally across the body in an uneven fold rather than a symmetrical drape — built entirely for the entrance walk.
#JustSpreehIt
Celebrations Simplified

posts.caption:
#Givenchy Spring 1997 was #AlexanderMcqueens first collection after taking over from #JohnGalliano. It actually received mixed reactions, many felt McQueen was too young and too rebellious for a house built on traditional French elegance.
His appointment represented a huge shift in fashion, as younger designers began taking control of historic couture houses. McQueen himself later criticized the collection, but over time it became one of the most important moments in his career.
#Zendaya in @givenchy Spring 1987 and accompanying @philiptreacy headpiece, styled by @luxurylaw
posts.alt_text:
Father & son duo, @jadhobeika and @georgeshobeika unveiled a glittering display of the best of #Lebanese craftsmanship and codes of Parisian haute couture for their fall 2026 collection. 

#HauteCouture #Fashion #Runway

#Givenchy Spring 1997 was #AlexanderMcqueens first collection after taking over from #JohnGalliano. It actually received mixed reactions, many felt McQueen was too young and too rebellious for a house built on traditional French elegance. 

His appointment represented a huge shift in fashion, as younger designers began taking control of historic couture houses. McQueen himself later criticized the collection, but over time it became one of the most important moments in his career.

posts.caption:
For his Fall 2026 #Schiaparelli Couture collection, #DanielRoseberry explained that he had deliberately travelled to #Barcelona in the hope that the city would inspire his new collection.
One of the collection’s key influences was the work of #AntoniGaudí and his distinctive architecture. Gaudí’s influence can be seen throughout several looks, particularly those inspired by the façade of #CasaBatlló and the sun mosaic at Park Güell.
Schiaprelli used intricate embroidery and embellishment, including real flowers that were fed sugar water to preserve their colour and flexibility before being carefully embroidered onto the garments. Echoing Gaudí’s iconic mosaic techniques and organic forms, transforming architectural details into wearable art.
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
Ahead of #JonathanAnderson’s second couture debut, step inside the salons of 30 Avenue Montaigne and rediscover the work of #ChristianDior in the 1950s, a period that defined an era of fashion and cemented #Paris as the capital of #HauteCouture.
#Dior became renowned for his ability to reinvent the female silhouette almost every season. Throughout the 1950s, he continued to develop the New Look with its tiny, corseted waist, rounded shoulders, and full calf-length skirts crafted from luxurious silks, satins, taffetas, and organza.
posts.alt_text:
#Givenchy Spring 1997 was #AlexanderMcqueens first collection after taking over from #JohnGalliano. It actually received mixed reactions, many felt McQueen was too young and too rebellious for a house built on traditional French elegance. 

His appointment represented a huge shift in fashion, as younger designers began taking control of historic couture houses. McQueen himself later criticized the collection, but over time it became one of the most important moments in his career.

posts.caption:
Founded in 1838, @charvet_official was one of the world’s first dedicated shirtmaker. It pioneered the concept of luxury bespoke shirting and has long been recognised as one of the most discreet and respected maisons.
A full circle moment, the shirts hold a special place in #GabrielleChanel’s own history. Her great love and inspiration, Arthur “Boy” Capel, was known to wear #Charvet shirts, and #Chanel famously drew on masculine tailoring to redefine women’s fashion.
For his debut @chanelofficial collection, #MatthieuBlazy continued his signature of elevating everyday essentials through exceptional craftsmanship and collaborated with Charvet on shirts, each finished with pearl buttons and Chanel’s signature chain sewn into the hem.
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
One of my favourite details from #Jacquemus’ sun-soaked show in Corsica was the series of headscarves that became part of the garments themselves. These looks embodied @simon_porte_jacquemus’ vision of Mediterranean dressing.
A close-fitting hood was tied at the nape of the neck before flowing down the back as a long panel of fabric, draping into a cowl seamlessly transforming into a dress or coat. It was a sculptural silhouette that feels both minimalist and dramatic, relying on fluid construction.
They also recall the practical headscarves traditionally worn by women across the #Mediterranean, the draped silhouettes of ancient #Greek and #Roman dress, and veils seen in religious imagery.

posts.caption:
When the heels finally come off on the French Riviera, true comfort takes over 💆‍♀️✨
📸 @maisonbentofficial
As part of my ultimate luxury survival guide for Cannes Lions, winding down intentionally is just as important as showing up. I am absolutely living in my pink satin striped PETER ALEXANDER pajama set and matching slippers for my hotel room downtime.
As captured in the image there is nothing quite like catching a quiet, slow morning on a balcony overlooking the beautiful South of France with a warm cup of coffee before the chaos of panels and yacht networking begins. They are lightweight, incredibly chic, and perfect for hot summer nights when you still want to look entirely put together.
Luxury isn't just what you wear on the red carpet; it’s how you treat yourself when the cameras stop flashing. ☕🥐

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
Celine might have just convinced me that silk scarves deserve a permanent spot in everyone’s wardrobe.
The house’s latest campaign feels like a study in movement, color, styling, and actually building a world around a product. From scarves draped as head coverings to windswept flags and even wrapped around a Dalmatian, every image finds a new way to make a familiar accessory feel unexpectedly modern.
Needless to say, my summer scarf obsession has officially begun.
#Celine
posts.alt_text:
Couture is often used as a catch-all term in fashion to describe opulent fashion, but authentic couture (called Haute Couture, which translates from “high sewing” in French) is much more complex. In France, Haute Couture isn’t just a type of collection, but a legally protected title regulated by the French Ministry of Industry and fiercely policed by the Chambre Syndicale de la Haute Couture. It’s a structural gauntlet so severe that only a permanent inner circle holds full sovereign status, while foreign powerhouses are relegated to “corresponding” members, and independent designers enter the calendar strictly by temporary guest invitations. And while primary shows take place twice a year in Paris, the couture ecosystem also extends to other foreign and non-syndicated designations like Alta Moda.

It’s a lot to keep up with, but I’ve gathered everything all in one place for you here: from the history of couture and how it saved thousands of garment workers from Nazi Germany, the essential legal conditions and vocabulary for syndicated and non-syndicated couture, what goes into producing a collection, and who’s buying couture—and why.

Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com

#FashionSubstack #HauteCouture #Schiaparelli #Dior #Chanel

posts.caption:
Schiaparelli is officially back, and Daniel Roseberry opened Paris Couture Week with one of the season’s most anticipated collections… but what else is new?
Titled The Abyss, the FW26 Haute Couture collection embraced an aquatic, surrealist vision, trading rigid couture formulas for the kind of fantasy that’s become synonymous with Roseberry’s tenure at Schiaparelli.
And he positioned the collection as a response to the growing influence of AI in fashion, arguing for the enduring value of human emotion, imagination, and extraordinary craftsmanship (not to mention attention to detail).
A very strong opening to couture week and another reminder that the future of fashion may be technological, but couture remains deeply human.
Comment your thoughts—and if you want to read more about the world of Haute Couture, check out our “Study” section on Substack.
#Schiaparelli #DanielRoseberry #HauteCouture

I’ll spare you the endless wedding discourse in case your feed needs a breather. Instead, let’s focus on getting some UV, embracing our Ralph Lauren Americana lifestyle, and catching up on your new favorite fashion Substack… ahem, The Citizen’s Poste.
Cheers to that, and happy weekending.

posts.caption:
NYFW is officially on the calendar. The CFDA has released the preliminary Spring/Summer 2027 schedule, running September 10–15th, with over 70 shows and presentations set to take over the city.
The usual anchors are in place—Tory Burch, Michael Kors, Carolina Herrera—holding down the legacy end of the week.
Say it with me: “Gerd your loins!”
#NYFW #CFDA #NewYorkFashionWeek #SS27
posts.alt_text:
Celine might have just convinced me that silk scarves deserve a permanent spot in everyone’s wardrobe.

The house’s latest campaign feels like a study in movement, color, styling, and actually building a world around a product. From scarves draped as head coverings to windswept flags and even wrapped around a Dalmatian, every image finds a new way to make a familiar accessory feel unexpectedly modern.

Needless to say, my summer scarf obsession has officially begun.

#Celine

posts.caption:
Long before gelato became a personality type and desserts started arriving in clean-label tubs, cassata ice cream had already perfected the drama.
Inspired by the Italian cassata dessert, it became something entirely its own in India: sponge cake, tutti-frutti, pista, strawberry, and synthetic colour, all stacked into one slice that felt fancier than it had any right to.
For many Indians, cassata ice cream was childhood luxury. Wedding buffets, old-school parlours, family dinners, freezer boxes opened for guests. A dessert that looked like occasion, even before anyone knew how to describe it.
Full story at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#FoodHistory #Cassata #IndianDesserts #Nostalgia #Culture
posts.alt_text:
Rahul Mishra’s (@rahulmishra_7) couture is easiest to understand through the designer he has always tried to be: a storyteller who sees fashion as an ecosystem of labour, memory, craft, and belief.

That impulse has shaped his work for years, from his commitment to artisan communities to the way he has steadily expanded the language of Indian craft. Those who have watched Mishra move from Indian fashion weeks to winning the International Woolmark Prize and now showing at the Paris Haute Couture Week have also watched a designer grow increasingly confident in his own vocabulary.

That confidence sits at the centre of his latest couture collection, DEVI: The Eternal Muse. Mishra is no longer simply translating Indian history for an international room. He is excavating it. The collection experiments with trompe-l’œil surfaces that suggest sandstone, basalt, and bronze rather than fabric. What holds it together is the way Mishra makes cloth carry the weight of stone. Zardozi, dabka, crystals, stones, and bugle beads build depth and texture, turning embroidery into architecture and couture into moving sculpture.

And yet, DEVI is where Mishra’s defining strength begins to reveal its limits. The handwork is extraordinary, but the idea sometimes becomes too literal, too heavily loaded. The goddess, the muse, the artefact, the monument: the woman is asked to hold so much symbolism that she risks becoming less human than icon.

What the collection reveals is not whether Indian couture can hold its own in Paris. That argument is already dated. Mishra’s craft is never in question. What it needs now is risk: a willingness to let beauty become less obedient, less reverent, and perhaps more uncomfortable.

📸: Chloé Rasquinet (@lookwhatyoumademesee), Arina Prikhodko (@arineeel), Gourab Ganguli (@gourabganguli), Moral moda (@moralmoda_visionnaire), karlaotto (@karlaotto) ,silxstaryst, Mateusz Pielesz (@mateuszpielesz)

posts.caption:
The old celebrity deal was a face on a campaign. The new one is a face on the cap table. For years, Indian celebrities have helped consumer brands borrow trust, speed up discovery, and make products feel culturally inevitable. Kareena Kapoor Khan’s (@kareenakapoorkha) investment in Fizzy Goblet (@fizzygoblet) is best read against the financial value and earning power of her public image. In 2024, Kapoor Khan’s brand value was estimated at $79.5 million by Kroll.
Kapoor Khan’s move from endorsement to equity asks why that value should end at a campaign fee. Her earlier bets, from Pluckk to Quench Botanics, follow the same logic, revealing an understanding of the consumer economy orbiting her: the food women want to eat healthier, the skincare they are curious about, the festive fashion they want to be seen in.
With Fizzy Goblet, that equation becomes cleaner. The amount remains undisclosed, but the value of the deal is not limited to the cheque. It converts a familiar celebrity association into a growth signal at a moment when the brand is chasing scale, retail expansion, and sharper consumer recall. Kapoor Khan has already helped make Fizzy Goblet more visible and more legible to a consumer who wants Indian craft without the heaviness of heritage retail.
The investment reflects a broader shift in how influence is being monetised in India. Celebrity image is now being treated as an asset class, with equity as the more ambitious end of the deal.

posts.caption:
BL drama has moved from Asian fandoms to Indian screens, fan pages, and Instagram series.
With Romil and Jugal, Mujhe Rang De, and The Visitor, the genre is making queer romance more visible, more watchable, and harder to treat as a side plot. The audience is there too: Rajvi Jhala of bl_imagines says interest is strongest among 25–34-year-olds, followed closely by 18–25-year-olds. Several episodes of Mujhe Rang De have also crossed one million views.
But visibility is not the same as acceptance. In a country where queer rights remain fragile, BL raises a more thornier question: is this a cultural shift, or does queer love still feel safest on screen?
Read more by Anant at the link in bio.
#BLdrama #QueerRepresentation #IndianBL #BoyLoveGenre #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Even before Sabyasachi (@sabyasachiofficial) became a global Indian luxury house, with jewellery, accessories, retail theatres, international flagships, and a vocabulary the world now recognises, the ambition was already travelling.
His work had reached Browns in London. He had shown at Milan Fashion Week. By the time he made his New York Fashion Week debut in 2006, Sabyasachi was not unknown, but he was not yet the force he is today.
The lesson, by his own admission, was timing. He had gone to America with craft, ambition, and a point of view, but without the full luxury ecosystem that could carry them at scale. So he built one at home.
Indian luxury now has the scale, confidence, and vocabulary to enter global fashion without translating itself down. The Sabyasachi that shows at NYFW now is no longer only the designer of bridal mythology and red-carpet saris. He arrives as an Indian luxury house choosing the stage rather than chasing it.
The choice of NYFW matters because this is not couture week. It places Indian craft inside the ready-to-wear conversation, where global fashion builds markets, categories, and cultural memory.

posts.caption:
Even if you know nothing about Masaba Gupta (@masabagupta), at the very least you know that she’s famous. Some people know her simply as Masaba, founder of the fashion label House of Masaba (@houseofmasaba) that, for the last 17 years, has been making playfully printed contemporary Indian clothing. Others know her as the star of the Netflix show ‘Masaba Masaba’. To the chronically online, she’s the powerful influencer, the person brands go to when they want a cool, articulate personality to represent them. The makeup-obsessed know her via LoveChild by Masaba (@lovechildbymasaba), the beauty brand she started in 2021, which has taken her obsession with makeup and turned it into products built specifically around Indian skin tones.
Yet, as she continues to do it all, there is a change underway. As we walk out of her office, Masaba casually drops a piece of news. “I’m moving away from ready-to-wear entirely. I think spring/summer 2026 is the last one we’ll do. The focus is going to be on occasion and bridalwear.” It’s a pretty big declaration, and a timely one, considering that later this month Masaba will be a part of the FDCI Hyundai India Couture Week in New Delhi. Right now, it feels like Masaba is moving into a new era, one where she’s more of a founder and businesswoman than a niche fashion designer.
At the link in bio, @butoolbegum spends the day in the company of the enterprising designer, business owner, and new mom.

posts.alt_text:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.

“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”

@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
There is a very specific kind of grief—the grief of a reader who has run out of books by their favourite author. And for the millions of people who found in Terry Pratchett something they could not find anywhere else, it is a grief that has lately, unexpectedly, grown louder.
If his worldview is this relevant, his books this good, and if they sell this well—over 100 million copies, why the sudden alarm? The answer is simple: no one has managed to put his work on a screen properly.There have been 11 screen adaptations of Pratchett’s work across 32 years, and not one has landed in pop culture the way it needed to.
At the link in bio, @_niddee_ chats with Pratchett fans about what his work means to them.

posts.caption:
Somewhere between wanting your lips to look undone and wanting the colour to survive the day, lip tints have become the only product delivering on both. They hold through iced coffees, wedding buffets, and decisions you’d rather not explain.
And the new generation has taken that brief somewhere far more interesting. We’re talking sheer jellies, tinted oils, and that K-beauty gradient flush that looks like you just bit into something very red and liked it.
At the link in bio, @priyankaparkash's edit of lip tints that have earned a permanent spot in your lip combo.

posts.caption:
some collections are inspired by history. GmbH’s ss27 collection is trying to recover it.
to mark its 10th anniversary, the Berlin-based label looked back at the city’s largely forgotten fashion legacy before the Nazi regime dismantled its thriving creative community. through archival research, historic references and collaborations with local makers, the collection becomes both a celebration of Berlin’s past and a reminder of everything that was lost.
one of the most thoughtful collections we came across this season.
#TFO #GmbH #BerlinFashionWeek #SS27 #FashionHistory
posts.alt_text:
Chanel said touch grass… so Matthieu Blazy built an entire enchanted garden instead

for his second Haute Couture outing, Blazy leaned fully into fantasy with giant flowers bursting through the runway, fairy-like silhouettes, organza tweed, floral embroidery and heels that looked more like tiny sculptural artworks than shoes

if couture is meant to transport you somewhere else, mission accomplished

#TFO #chanel #couture

posts.caption: beautiful, but where is the obsession? blazy’s chanel feels like archival vintage shops in paris and london filtered through an algorithm: halston, armani, issey miyake, bias cuts, 1930s bridal nostalgia, all dressed up with the bells and whistles of mid-2010 loewe’s idea of what ‘contemporary’ means today. the atelier delivers, but the house still needs a signature. couture clients don’t need more choices. they need the one thing only chanel can offer: desire.

posts.caption:
if there’s one accessory that’s become a year-round uniform, it’s a great dad cap, and few do it better than ’47.
the brand has just opened its first London pop-up on Newburgh Street in Soho, bringing its iconic MLB and NFL caps, seasonal drops and collaborations into one space. after more than 75 years of perfecting the silhouette, ’47 continues proving that the best staples don’t need to chase trends, they just need to come in every colour imaginable.
safe to say… choosing just one might be the hardest part.
#TFO #dadcap #fashion #47brand

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra just reminded everyone that couture is supposed to leave you speechless
his Devi collection celebrated Indian history, mythology and craftsmanship through breathtaking embroidery, sculptural silhouettes and ceremonial headpieces created with clay artist Sumant Kumar.
every look felt monumental, but there was one clear scene stealer. the now viral stone face headpiece that has fashion lovers talking all over social media
#TFO #rahulmishra #couture

posts.caption:
“For many years, Mishra has demonstrated that embroidery can interpret modern fine art, poetry and spirituality. With Devi , he suggested that embroidery could interpret sculpture. Layer upon painstaking layer of zardozi, beads, crystals, metallic threadwork and hand embroidery transformed fabric into surfaces that appeared chiselled rather than stitched.” Read Shefalee Vasudev’s review of Rahul Mishra’s Fall 2026 couture show Devi, that was showcased yesterday at Paris Haute Couture Week. Link in bio.
🖋️ @shefatwork
@rahulmishra_7

posts.caption:
With social media and e-commerce reshaping access to markets, digital empowerment of artisans has become as vital to preserving craft as the skills themselves. Introducing 𝑪𝒓𝒂𝒇𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑫𝒊𝒈𝒊𝒕𝒂𝒍 𝑭𝒖𝒕𝒖𝒓𝒆, a collaborative series between TVOF and the Digital Empowerment Foundation, that organises the annual Digital Artisans of India awards, in conjunction with Digikargha and Creative Dignity. Osama Manzar and Meera Goradia kickstart this series spotlighting artisans using digital power to preserve craft, expand opportunity and imagine a more equitable future for India’s handmade traditions. posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com
posts.alt_text:
Rembrandt once gave us instructions on how to view his work….”Don’t stand too close.” he said. 

This week was session 3 of @readingsbooks Big Book Club on The Odyssey and we learned a little history about oral poetry. 

Dr Tom Hercules Davies, a comparative philologist at @unimelb actually sung a few lines from the poem to help us understand the important of the musicality of the text. 

Genuinely, it was so fascinating! 

Read the full essay via the link on my bio 

In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption:
I didn’t know I could love Madame Grès any more, but here we are.
As much as I love her designs and her meticulous work (and they are mind flowingly beautiful), I would give anything to go to her atelier when she was working, sit down and talk to her…that’s if she even would have let me.
There’s something about her that I connect with. Maybe it was her penchant for expensive fabrics and her lack of budgeting. Maybe it was her ability to stand up for herself when everything around her was trying to tear her down. Or maybe it was her steadfast attitude towards her work.
I also love that her friends, Cristobal Balenciaga, Yves Saint Laurent and Pierre Cardin, banded together helped her out when she was struggling financially. How sweet is that?
All I know was that despite having a sad end, she lived a fascinating life.
Swipe to learn more about this fascinating womann and watch the full YouTube video about her life now. I also drape on the mannequin and have a bit of a moment of connection with her after doing so.

posts.caption: It's a bit idiotic to think that some cherry picked strong women in the Odyssey would mean that homer was a women. Firstly, many strong "women" are just Gods e.g Athena, Hera e.t.c. Also, the Iliad is centered around Helen, a women who could do nothing in the face of Paris's seduction. In the book where Paris is rescued by Aphrodite and placed in her bed chamber, She calls herself a whore for abandoning Menelaus. Also, Women in the Iliad are treated as mere commodities whose purpose is to be won or lost. Agmmemnnon takes chryses from Apollo's priest and the women can do nothing. Only when Apollo himself intervenes does Atrides releases her and then as soon as he releases her, he takes a chillies beloved slavegirl Brieses which causes the rift between him and a chillies. If homer was a women, it would have been in his interest to portray these women as something more than just merchandise. Even if "she" didn't want to do it as so not to paint an idealistic picture, the writer could have at least shown or written more from their perspective but we get almost nothing. Only the goddesses are seen as strong and independent which was the common view in his time. The evidence points in the direction of a man or a collection of scribes which were primarily men during homers times. Also the etymology of the word homer doesn't suggest anything feminine.

posts.caption:
Are you doing enough cardio? The metric that measures cardio effectiveness, VO₂ max, may sound like something out of chemistry class, but it's actually an important component of living a long, healthy life. Longevity expert @DrVicenteMera points out that to get the most out of physical exercise, “you need to work on strength, cardio, and stretching without prioritizing one over the other.” Tap the link in bio to learn more about the direct link between VO₂ max and longevity, and how this could impact your workout routine.
Photographed by Steven Klein, Vogue, September 2003

posts.caption:
Our latest menswear size inclusivity report reveals that runway representation during the latest SS27 men’s season remains largely unchanged. Alongside the continued dominance of straight-size casting, conversations were shaped by the return of extreme body ideals — from hyper-muscular physiques to the comeback of ultra-slim silhouettes.
Of the 2,741 looks presented across Milan Fashion Week and Paris Fashion Week, just 0.2% featured plus-size models, unchanged from last season, while mid-size representation rose to 2.3%. At the link in bio, @AmyFrancombe_ breaks down the findings from the full report.
posts.alt_text: Big Tech is investing in fashion marketing to give AI smart glasses cultural appeal — but consumers still see them as synonymous with surveillance. As brands turn to celebrity campaigns, creators, and fashion-led launches to drive adoption, tech editor @AmyEOBrien explores what it will take for AI wearables to win over skeptical consumers, at the link in bio.

posts.caption: As someone that actually has to work with models on their body and size requirements... who made this up? Or is this post focussed on the USA which already was not focussed on skinny male models. All the male models I have coached for this season still had to be skinny, still had the same body requirements, there were a few exceptions made for models with a higher social media presence. I do not recognise this at all in my daily work with high fashion models in Europe. The models that did not have to have those strict standards walked for shows that already do not have those standards.
posts.alt_text: Big Tech is investing in fashion marketing to give AI smart glasses cultural appeal — but consumers still see them as synonymous with surveillance. As brands turn to celebrity campaigns, creators, and fashion-led launches to drive adoption, tech editor @AmyEOBrien explores what it will take for AI wearables to win over skeptical consumers, at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Size inclusivity in menswear remains largely static across #MilanFashionWeek and #ParisFashionWeek, with this season also welcoming more polarized body ideals. Alongside the continued dominance of straight-size casting, there was a sharp focus on extreme physiques, from "protein chic" and hyper-muscular builds shaped by looksmaxxing, to the return of ultra-slim "slender man" silhouettes. We found that of the thousands of looks presented across Milan and Paris SS27 shows, only 0.2% were shown on plus-size models, remaining unchanged since FW26. Tap the link in bio for a break down of the full report.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: If you thought yesterday's fresh-off-the-@Schiaparelli couture runway ensemble was the wildest "Odyssey" fashion moment we'd get from @Zendaya, you would be severely mistaken. Today in Paris, the star traded the newest of new for a serious archive pull from Alexander McQueen's spring 1997 couture collection for @Givenchy. Tap the link in our bio for more details.
