posts.caption:
For their first Vogue India cover together, Padmanabh Singh (@pachojaipur) and Gauravi Kumari (@gauravikumari) turn a day in Singapore into a sibling sport. Born one year apart, they grew up like twins with very different operating systems. Gauravi is “the planner, the pseudo-mom”, while Padmanabh continues to baffle her with spontaneous travel plans and a living-out-of-a-suitcase lifestyle.
Read the July-August cover story at the link in bio.
Head of Editorial Content: Rochelle Pinto (@rochelle.pinto)
Photographer: Zantz Han (@zantz)
Stylist: Manglien Gangte (@manglien)
Words by: Lamiya Chitalwalla (@lamiyachitalwalla)
Hair: Zhou Aiyi (@zhouaiyimua)
Makeup: Victoria Hwang (@vichwangmua)
Bookings Editor: Aliza Fatma (@alizaafatmaa)
Entertainment Director: Megha Mehta (@magzmehta)
Producer: Emily Teng (@eemileet)
Location Courtesy: Singapore Tourism Board (@visit_singapore)
Hospitality Partner: Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore (@frasershousesingapore)
On Gauravi: Dress, MAISON MARGIELA (@maisonmargiela). Clash de Cartier earrings, Long Clash de Cartier necklace, Juste un Clou bracelet, Juste un Clou classic bracelet, Juste un Clou ring, all CARTIER (@cartier).
On Padmanabh: Jacket, trousers; both HERMÈS (@hermes). Panthère de Cartier watch, Clash de Cartier bracelet; both CARTIER (@cartier).

On a simpler note, @athiyashetty picked a @houseofmasaba salwar suit in a silk tissue fabric and Anshula tied it all together in a peach @taruntahiliani lehenga.

#VogueFashion

posts.caption: “Borrowed from the boys,” an oft-used fashion phrase, usually refers to women adopting menswear staples as their own. But there’s far more to borrow from the men’s runways than tailoring. This June’s spring 2027 menswear shows were packed with styling ideas worth stealing for the summer ahead. Shop these looks at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Ask anyone, and they'll tell you that dressing for New York City heat is an acquired skill, but Vogue's editors have mastered the art. Senior editor @MaiMorsch continues to reach for this look from Comme Si because it's as comfortable as pajamas yet polished enough for the office. Meanwhile, fashion news writer @Alexandra.Hildreth prioritizes breathable materials and easy silhouettes to stay true to her all-black wardrobe, while associate editor Nicole Martini (@just_martini) gravitates toward lightweight pieces that feel effortless from morning to night. Shop the full summer in the city edit at the link in bio. Photographed by @theochoi_

posts.caption: Sometimes, there’s a clear cause for celebration—a birthday, a new job, a housewarming. Sometimes, the reason for a gathering is a little more modest in origin—for example, a fresh crop of mandarins sprouting in your backyard. “Today, our tree gave up a small mountain of tangerines,” says @KendallKnox, the co-founder of L.A.-based interiors brand @OliveAteliers. “I decided to lean all the way into the European summer we’re all quietly craving.” Knox put together a colorful cornucopia of early summer treats inspired by what’s in season at her local farmer’s market and in her own garden. For a little inspiration for the holiday weekend, head to the link in our bio to see how she pulled it all together.

posts.caption:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.
With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces.
For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.
Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo
Photography: @chou_mo_ | @overface_artists

Set assistant: @itsmrcole
Floral design: @temperature_official
Production: @overface_production
Jewellery: @tiffanyandco
Talent: @chua.tanya
Story: @ehnyway
posts.alt_text:
Curiousity never killed the cat. So at 51, @chua.tanya is still doing hardcore gymnastics; an indisputable reflection of what has always been a lifelong resolve—to always try the things that scare you. 

"I’m doing backflips and handstands at an age where most people would be terrified to try. But if you’re constantly fearful of failure, nothing is going to get done. You’ll never do anything interesting or out of the ordinary," our 'Audacity' cover star proclaims. 

In the link in bio, the Singaporean singer-songwriter's narrative may read mercurial yet there was always a steadfast nerve behind it all: be it back when she debuted with her English album in 1997, when she wanted to do a tour dedicated to her less recognised tracks or even when she decided to take up gymnastics later in life. 

The July/Aug 'Audacity' issue is available for pre-order, and will hit newsstands on 22 July. 

Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo
Photography: @chou_mo_ | @overface_artists

posts.caption:
From @Madonna’s pink bridal veil for her surprise @YSL afterparty rave to @KatseyeWorld’s #Coachella performance and @Beyonce’s Cowboy Carter tour, @AgroStudio_ have become the designer duo behind some of pop’s most memorable wardrobes.
Founded by @anguxx and @geoxby, at the heart of Agro Studio lies an instinctive understanding of the body—creating pieces that transcend aesthetics, identities and silhouettes. Whether working with Chantilly lace, Mongolian fur or crystal corsetry, the duo’s technical fluency allows them to craft a distinct visual language for every wearer.
Alongside an exclusive behind-the-scenes video from their fall/winter 2026 collection ‘The Wanderer,’ the designer duo speaks with #VogueSingapore’s digital writer @agnesmshu about building Agro Studio, the influence of commercial work on their creative language, and taking ego out of the process. Read the full interview in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Few names are as recognisable as @dlwlrma's—her quiet artistry a statement of its own. It is precisely this tireless yet refined approach to all of her disciplines that makes her a perfect fit for her next role: @jaegerlecoultre's newest ambassador. Announced through 'The Hour Before', an intimate interview concept that bridges the worlds of the storied maison and the artist in question, the South Korean starlet reveals snippets of her creative universe, and the wondrous link-up that arrives with this ambassadorship.
As for #IU's favourite #JaegerLeCoultre watch? "Definitely the Reverso. I've always loved its classic design and distinctive beauty, which is why it's my favourite. Wearing it for this campaign made me realise that it suits me rather well, which made me happy. Anyone who looks good in a Reverso is cool, isn’t it?" #VogueSingapore #TheHourBefore

posts.caption: Dear unbelieving neighbour who is reading this now, whether you are a man or a woman, a Jew or a Gentile: Are you a good person? Not according to your own opinion, but according to the holy Law of God, your sovereign Creator and righteous Judge (Romans 3:10–20). No one is, for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God (Romans 3:23). Truly repent, confess your sins to God, and believe by faith alone in the Lord Jesus Christ, crucified and risen, who is the only Savior and Mediator between the holy God and sinful humanity (Romans 10:9–13; Ephesians 2:8–9). Only then, by sovereign grace, will you be freely justified, reconciled to God, delivered from God’s eternal wrath in hell, and empowered by the Holy Spirit to live a life of holiness and good works pleasing to God, living for His eternal glory (Ephesians 2:10; James 2:26; Romans 5:1; 8:1). “Therefore, we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God were making His appeal through us; we implore you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God. He made Him who knew no sin to be sin for us, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God” (2 Corinthians 5:20–21). I lovingly invite you, in Christ, to reflect on this message by watching this short video: https://youtu.be/pYZw0dfLmLk

posts.alt_text: Big Tech is investing in fashion marketing to give AI smart glasses cultural appeal — but consumers still see them as synonymous with surveillance. posts.caption: Based on the monolithic standing stones in his native Ireland, @standing_ground_ designer Michael Stewart presented a collection of sculpted corsets and fluid drapery during the fall 2026 couture collections. “It’s all very elaborate work, in the sense that just to make the simplicity come through,” he told Vogue’s Sarah Mower during a preview at his London atelier. “It has to have the elaborate elements to kind of get to that level.” Click the link in our bio to see every look.

posts.caption: It is fascinating to see the folding fan enjoying a level of popularity we haven’t seen since the 18th century. Jonathan Anderson’s Autumn 2026 Haute Couture collection for Dior beautifully reminds us how this accessory has long captivated the masters, from Lagerfeld’s Chanel signature to the Spanish-infused archives of Gaultier, Armani, and Balmain.Anderson’s decade at Loewe clearly connected him to Iberian craftsmanship, and his eye for elevating these pleated marvels is undeniable. However, as a Spaniard, seeing this trend hit the runway sparks a sweet feeling but also a cultural appropriation in occident . While high fashion rediscovers it in cycles, the real reason the fan survived is that our grandmothers and mothers kept it alive as a daily cultural tool—not just for the heat, but for romance, social connection, fashion and self expresion and even an unique code of courtship that my grandma knew! With global warming now making heatwaves a global reality, the world is rediscovering its utility, but for us, it will always be a beautiful, breathing piece of our culture and intimate everyday heritage since ever. 🩵

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra @rahulmishra_7 presented ‘Devi’ at Haute Couture Week, Paris. Drawing inspiration from ancient South Indian sculptures and the divine feminine, the collection was an absolute showstopper.
The lineup effortlessly transformed featherlight fabrics into breathtaking, structured silhouettes. By blending traditional artistry like intricate hand embroidery, zardozi, and dabka with modern design; Mishra beautifully celebrated India's rich artisanal heritage. The collection's contemporary edge was further elevated through brilliant collaborations with clay artisan Sumant Kumar, legendary milliner Stephen Jones, and Tanishq Natural Diamonds, seamlessly breathing new life into centuries-old craftsmanship.
Jewellery by @tanishqjewellery
Visit www.weddingvows.com for latest updates in wedding trends, region based vendors and bridal inspiration.

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.
photographer: @gleesonpaulino
stylist: @laurenegg
creative direction: @arrrrmani
hair stylist: @laurenpalmersmith
makeup artist: @jentioseco
manicurist: @thuybnguyen
entertainment director: @jbake21
writer: @balencianas
producer: @_porcelainchaos @delafe_pr
special thanks to @blackwidowmustang & @goldlinebar
posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto. 

The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project. 

In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Every once in a while a designer comes along who sets fashion editors’ hearts aflutter.
In a week when Pierpaolo Piccioli is scheduled to show his first haute couture collection at @Balenciaga, and @DuranLantinkyo will make his couture debut at @JeanPaulGaultier, a small independent designer from London has made his presence felt.
Since winning the inaugural Savoir-Faire award at the 2024 @LVMH Prize, Michael Stewart had not shown his creations in Paris. Anna Wintour was among the 150 guests who gathered at the Irish embassy for the debut of his @Standing_Ground_ label at Paris Couture Week.
Stewart said he spent the 200,000-euro prize money — and then some — on perfecting his scifi-infused designs for fashion’s biggest stage.
Read the full collection review at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Review: @jdiderich
